commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=20f730f8b32c4813e95a0942a66bfe0dd9f4ece1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds source-wide memory/session embedding batching, OpenAI-compatible remote batch support, runtime batch API fields, verbose batching logs, and regression tests for memory indexing.

PR surface: Source +777, Tests +382. Total +1159 across 13 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the main blocker: source comparison against current main shows the branch's batch runner returns raw `pollIntervalMs` where current main clamps and tests assert the safe behavior. The PR's CLI proof shows the intended small source-wide batch path, but not the large 833+657 file case from the comments.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review is needed because the remaining blockers include a public plugin runtime API decision plus rebase safety around current main's batch-runner behavior.

target.title: perf(memory): batch memory embeddings across files
target.body:
## Summary

- Batch-enabled source-wide embedding now defers memory and session source indexing into one shared queue before submitting provider batches.
- OpenAI-compatible source-wide verbose logs now print the batching path, source distribution, file count, chunk count, group, and flush reason.
- The regression test now requires forced memory+session indexing to use one provider runtime batch instead of separate per-source batches.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** `openclaw memory index --force --verbose` should not submit separate OpenAI-compatible embedding batches for memory files and session files when both sources are enabled; the verbose output should also identify the source-wide batching path.
- **Real environment tested:** Real OpenClaw CLI from `/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238`, isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, 3 memory markdown files, 2 session transcript files, provider `openai`, model `test-embed`, batch enabled, local OpenAI-compatible batch HTTP endpoint on `127.0.0.1:18083`.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
cd /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-88238
OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/runtime-home \
NO_COLOR=1 \
pnpm openclaw memory index --force --verbose \
  > /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-88238-evidence/openclaw-memory-index.log 2>&1
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
Memory Index (main)
Sources: memory (MEMORY.md + $OPENCLAW_HOME/workspace/memory/*.md), sessions ($OPENCLAW_HOME/.openclaw/agents/main/sessions/*.jsonl)
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch prepare files=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 maxFiles=1024 maxChunks=4096
[memory] embeddings: source-wide batch submit group=1 source=memory+sessions files=5 chunks=5 sources=memory=3,sessions=2 reason=end
[memory] embeddings: openai batch submit
[memory] embeddings: openai batch created
Memory index updated (main).
```

```json
{
  "uploads": [
    {
      "fileId": "file-1",
      "requests": 5,
      "inputs": [
        "# Log\nAlpha memory line.\nZebra memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nBeta memory line.\n",
        "# Log\nGamma memory line.\n",
        "User: Session alpha memory line.",
        "Assistant: Session beta memory line."
      ]
    }
  ],
  "batches": [
    {
      "batchId": "batch-1",
      "inputFileId": "file-1",
      "outputFileId": "output-1",
      "requests": 5
    }
  ],
  "statusPolls": [],
  "outputReads": [
    "output-1"
  ]
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** One OpenAI-compatible batch was created with 5 requests spanning both configured sources: 3 memory files and 2 session transcript files. The console output now makes the source-wide code path and source distribution visible.
- **What was not tested:** A live third-party embedding endpoint was not tested because this environment has no embedding base URL/API key/model variables. The real OpenClaw CLI and OpenAI-compatible provider path were tested against a local OpenAI-compatible batch endpoint.
- **Target test file:** `extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts`

## Regression Test Plan

```text
OPENCLAW_TEST_MEMORY_UNSAFE_REINDEX=1 node scripts/test-projects.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/index.test.ts src/plugins/openai-compatible-embedding-provider.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/generic-embedding-provider.bridge.test.ts
[test] passed 2 Vitest shards in 33.76s

pnpm tsgo:test:extensions
exit code 0
```

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The previous source-wide path still indexed memory files and session files through separate source sync calls. That made the OpenAI-compatible provider submit separate batches per source, and the verbose output did not expose enough source-wide batching details to distinguish that path from per-file or per-source batching.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
